[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: ‘Let’s stand together in the Gypsy and Travellers against rubbish campaign' - says Genty Lee | Travellers Times
[METADATA/INTRO]: ‘Let’s stand together in the Gypsy and Travellers against rubbish campaign' - says Genty Lee Genty Lee, Chair of The Romany-Gypsy Guild explains the reason and hope behind the “Gypsies and Travellers against rubbish campaign”....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: We had to literally watch our step as the field had been used as a toilet - and the amount of rubbish? I’ve personally never seen anything like it. The organisers had provided both toilets and bins, it was infuriating to see bins empty and rubbish strewn all over the fields and human excrement everywhere. Standing still looking around, for as far as the eye could see all there was, was rubbish and £1000 of pounds worth of tents, camping chairs and sleeping bags. It struck a raw nerve with me in that moment, how society like to personally make me feel bad for the mess a small minority of Travellers leave behind, I wondered who’s made to feel bad for this festival bomb site? It also occurred to me that this aftermath of waste, filth and rubbish wasn’t written about in any of the local new papers that I had seen. Yet it seems every week there’s a story on an unauthorised encampment and seldom about local authority not for filling their duty of site provision but instead the report is all about the rubbish that’s left. This in itself grates my nerves as it seems to be when crime is committed by a member from a minority group the crime is linked to the ethnicity, yet when a crime is committed by those of privilege it’s because of them as an individual and no mention of ethnicity least of all the persuasive writings, we read in the tabloids, that the ethnicity has played a part in the crime!

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2013-07-25
[TITLE]: Elyria man arrives home to find giant pile of trash in front yard – Morning Journal
[METADATA/INTRO]: ELYRIA — An empty dog food bag, hangers, stuffed animals, clothes and a ventilation hood for a stove were just a few of the objects in a mountain of garbage that turned up Thursday on the lawn of a home...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: ELYRIA — An empty dog food bag, hangers, stuffed animals, clothes and a ventilation hood for a stove were just a few of the objects in a mountain of garbage that turned up Thursday on the lawn of a home at 2810 West River Road North. “There’s plenty of stuff in there,” said homeowner Darrell Childers. “I found some mail, a headboard to a bed and some used needles, too.” Childers found the pile of garbage sitting off of his driveway when he returned home from work at 9:45 p.m. Wednesday. “My wife left the house at 9:15 and I was here at 9:45,” Childers said. “Somewhere in the half-hour the trash appeared.” Childers immediately began to search for answers. “I started digging through the trash,” he said. “I was able to find a lot of old mail.” Finding an address on the mail, Childers was able to locate where the trash had come from. By 10:15 p.m., he said he was at the address on Lincoln Street. “I went straight there as soon as I found the address,” Childers said. Childers said there was only one person at the house when he arrived: a painter who had no idea what was going on. “I asked if he was the landlord or if he knew anything about a lot of trash,” Childers said. “He didn’t know anything.” What Childers was able to locate at the home was a dump truck that he said was the perfect size to accommodate the amount of trash that had been dumped on his lawn. “It looked like it could have been like a foreclosure situation and that the landlord had decided to get all of the stuff out of the house,” he said. After returning to his home, Childers called the Elyria Police. He said the police officers were shocked by what they saw. “The officer said, ‘I was expecting bunch of bags of trash, not all of this free stuff,'” Childers said. “The city sanitation crew also came out. They said that a front-end loader would probably be needed because nothing is in bags.” Childers’ driveway is about a quarter-mile long. However, he does not believe that it was a case where someone believed the driveway to be an unused road. “There’s a mailbox at the front of the driveway,” he said. “They even dumped the stuff about 50 feet from a ‘Keep Out’ sign.” Childers, who has lived at the home for six years, said that he wants whoever dumped the trash to rectify the situation. “It’s not fair to me to have to clean it up,” he said. “It’s also not fair for the city to have to waste their time with it. Whoever did it should just come here and clean it up.”

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2017-05-18
[TITLE]: Travellers offer residents cut-price rubbish collection - and then DUMP the waste on woodland space before leaving their illegal campsite
[METADATA/INTRO]: TRAVELLERS have sparked fury by offering residents cut-price rubbish collection – before dumping it in woods. The group took cash from locals for a cheap tip-run to the council’s household recycling centre just three miles away....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: TRAVELLERS have sparked fury by offering residents cut-price rubbish collection – before dumping it in woods. 
 The group took cash from locals for a cheap tip-run to the council’s household recycling centre just three miles away. 
 But instead of disposing of the junk properly they dumped it on a large clearing behind their camp, hidden by trees. 
 Furious residents discovered the fly-tipping mountain after the group left their camp in Stockwood, Bristol. 
 It includes furniture, fridges, ovens, kitchen units, a Henry vacuum cleaner, an entire sofa – and even bin liners filled with cannabis plants. 
 Most read in news 
 Local resident Mark Thomas said the site was “a disgusting mess”.
